content.full_text:
June 13, 1977

Fay Short  
1118 Jefferson Street  
Delano, California 93215

Dear Ms. Short:

Thank you for your recent letter in which you asked whether or not the city manager must be included in the Conflict of Interest provisions of the Political Reform Act. Under Section 87200 of the Act, city managers must file such statements which are forwarded to our office. On the other hand, city managers are not included in the codes filed with our agency. Only city council members are included in such a code filed with our office.

However, a city manager must be included in the city codes filed with the city council, as are all other city employees who will be designated employees. Thus, when your codes are developed for your city's "designated employees," the city manager will be also covered. In the meantime, before the codes are developed, the city managers must file statements because of Section 87200.

I have asked the League of California Cities to provide you with copies of several codes already adopted by cities throughout the state.

Please call on me if you have any further questions.

Sincerely,

Robert M. Stern  
General Counsel
